publicationStmt.p: Transcribed from Bodleian Library, Lovelace Byron collection, Box 165, folios 88-91. Transcription by Lisa Gold (2022). TEI conversion for Sextant pipeline, 2026.

div.p: Ockham Thursday. 3 o'clock
My Dearest.-- I am now lying on my dressing-room sofa, having come from my bed an hour ago.
Tomorrow I shall be getting about again as usual, & probably driving out.-- Had I not been so quiet, I think the disposition to faintness & exhaustion has
been so great this time, that I should have suffered very much indeed.

I mean to go to Town on Tuesday, & propose coming out with ou on Thursday afternoon.-- I shall be very careful to arrange not to have so much to do & excite me.-- Write me a letter that I may have in Town when I get there.--
My plan for next month, (with ou appro- -bation), is on no account

to give out that I shall be in London at all. I go only for three or four days at a time, sometimes leaving you in London & you sometimes coming to Ockham with me for my day or two of airing, & taking care always to call on those I wish to see, the day before going out of London, so that I may say I have only been for two or 3 nights.-- I think we must not move our establishment. I

propose that the kitchen-maid should go to remain, & perhaps one man, but no more.-- In this manner let me spend if you like, 5 or 6 weeks, avoiding all excitement for me as much as possible.-- I should then not take even Byron, unless for a day or two.
I intend to leave much in the house palpably unfinished & unfurnished, though I have though of many nice things to talk to ou about.-- I am quite vexed that the leather
will not do for the invalid sofa, since you so much like it. But at any rate I think that my sitting room sofa, which you know will be of the ordinary kind, should have it.
The green & crimson stripe is ordered for the common covers of the back drawing-room.
I have not yet made up my mind about a table for my sitting-room, to stand near my sofa. Probably for the moment I shall leave one of those out of
the back drawing-room.--

I am half tempted eventually to have that beautiful small Japan bird table, to stand in the middle of the room, but there is no hurry for anything of this kind, & I hope ou sees that I am a prudent avis.--
Have you yet at all settled how the mate-room is to be furnished? this spring I think we had better only put in a table & side-table, just as a termporary dining apparatus, & perhaps
a carpet.--

Byron is exceedingly mischievous, & more independent than every. The baby grows more beautiful [double underlined] than ever. I wonder how she will strike you after this absence.-- Dr Locock is anxious to see her, & says he expects to admire her.-- He asked much about the children, and was kind to the avis, whom I think he pitied for being without her mate to fondle her.--
She thinks much about ou, & how little she
deserves ou tenderness.--
Will ou eat her up, when ou sees her again?--
She wants to fly into ou neck, & nestle up to ou very much indeed. Ou won't hurt her I think, will ou?--
On Saturday I believe a horse is likely to arrive. it is a pity the dingy grey became so lame, for he was beginning to suit me well.--
